109	// NewManager creates a new Manager.
110	// It panics if EventCh is set without Ctx, as the event-injection goroutine requires
111	// a context to avoid leaking on shutdown.
112	func NewManager(cfg ManagerConfig) Manager {
113		if cfg.EventCh != nil && cfg.Ctx == nil {
114			panic("waf.ManagerConfig: Ctx must be set when EventCh is set")
115		}
116		return &pollerManager{
117			logger:                 cfg.Logger,
118			fetcher:                cfg.Fetcher,
119			deployments:            cfg.Deployments,
120			pollers:                make(map[types.NamespacedName]*pollerEntry),
121			pollErrors:             make(map[types.NamespacedName]*PollError),
122			bundleUpdates:          make(map[types.NamespacedName]BundleUpdate),
123			bundleCache:            make(map[graph.WAFBundleKey]*graph.WAFBundleData),
124			bundleKeyToPolicy:      make(map[graph.WAFBundleKey]types.NamespacedName),
125			bundleKeyToDescription: make(map[graph.WAFBundleKey]string),
126			statusCallback:         cfg.StatusCallback,
127			eventCh:                cfg.EventCh,
128			ctx:                    cfg.Ctx,
129		}
130	}
131	
132	// Config contains configuration for reconciling a poller.
133	type Config struct {
134		InitialChecksums  map[graph.WAFBundleKey]string
135		PolicyNsName      types.NamespacedName
136		Sources           []BundleSource
137		TargetDeployments []types.NamespacedName
138	}
139	
140	// ReconcilePoller ensures a poller is running with the correct configuration.
141	// If no poller exists, one is started. If a poller exists with the same sources,
142	// only the target deployments are updated. If sources have changed, the poller is restarted.
143	// This avoids unnecessary poller restarts when only targets change.
144	func (m *pollerManager) ReconcilePoller(ctx context.Context, cfg Config) {
145		if len(cfg.Sources) == 0 {
146			m.logger.V(1).Info("No polling sources, not starting poller", "policy", cfg.PolicyNsName)
147			return
148		}
149	
150		m.mu.Lock()
151		entry, exists := m.pollers[cfg.PolicyNsName]
152	
153		// If poller exists and sources haven't changed, just update targets if needed.
154		if exists && sourcesEqual(entry.poller.getSources(), cfg.Sources) {
155			m.mu.Unlock()
156			entry.poller.updateTargetDeployments(cfg.TargetDeployments)
157			return
158		}
159		m.mu.Unlock()
160	
161		// Sources changed or poller doesn't exist - need to (re)start.
162		m.startPoller(ctx, cfg)
163	}
164	
165	// startPoller starts a new poller, stopping any existing one first.
166	func (m *pollerManager) startPoller(ctx context.Context, cfg Config) {
167		m.mu.Lock()
168		defer m.mu.Unlock()
169	
170		// Always cancel any existing poller before overwriting the map entry.
171		// This is safe even if the caller didn't observe one, because another
172		// goroutine may have started one between our check and acquiring this lock.
173		if entry, exists := m.pollers[cfg.PolicyNsName]; exists {
174			m.logger.V(1).Info("Stopping existing poller before starting new one", "policy", cfg.PolicyNsName)
175			entry.cancel()
176			delete(m.pollErrors, cfg.PolicyNsName)
177			delete(m.bundleUpdates, cfg.PolicyNsName)
178			m.clearBundleCacheLocked(entry.poller)
179		}
180	
181		pollerCtx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
182	
183		var poller *poller
184	
185		// Create a wrapped callback that records poll results and triggers a status update
186		// scoped to just the poller's target deployments.
187		wrappedCallback := func(
188			policyNsName types.NamespacedName,
189			bundleKey graph.WAFBundleKey,
190			newChecksum string,
191			err error,
192		) {
193			m.mu.RLock()
194			desc := m.bundleKeyToDescription[bundleKey]
195			m.mu.RUnlock()
196			if desc == "" {
197				desc = "WAF bundle"
198			}
199			m.recordPollResult(policyNsName, bundleKey, desc, newChecksum, err)
200			if m.statusCallback != nil {
201				m.statusCallback(poller.getTargetDeployments())
202			}
203		}
204	
205		// Record which policy owns each bundle key and its human-readable description.
206		for _, src := range cfg.Sources {
207			m.bundleKeyToPolicy[src.BundleKey] = cfg.PolicyNsName
208			desc := src.Description
209			if desc == "" {
210				desc = "WAF bundle"
211			}
212			m.bundleKeyToDescription[src.BundleKey] = desc
213		}
214	
215		poller = newPoller(pollerConfig{
216			logger:               m.logger,
217			policyNsName:         cfg.PolicyNsName,
218			sources:              cfg.Sources,
219			fetcher:              m.fetcher,
220			deployments:          m.deployments,
221			targetDeployments:    cfg.TargetDeployments,
222			initialChecksums:     cfg.InitialChecksums,
223			statusCallback:       wrappedCallback,
224			bundleUpdateCallback: m.cacheBundleUpdate,
225		})
226	
227		m.pollers[cfg.PolicyNsName] = &pollerEntry{
228			poller: poller,
229			cancel: cancel,
230		}
231	
232		go func() {
233			poller.run(pollerCtx)
234			// Clean up after poller exits.
235			m.mu.Lock()
236			// Only delete if this is still the same poller (could have been replaced).
237			if entry, exists := m.pollers[cfg.PolicyNsName]; exists && entry.poller == poller {
238				delete(m.pollers, cfg.PolicyNsName)
239			}
240			m.mu.Unlock()
241		}()
242	
243		m.logger.Info("Started WAF poller", "policy", cfg.PolicyNsName, "sourceCount", len(cfg.Sources))
244	}
245	
246	// recordPollResult records the result of a poll attempt for a policy.
247	// If err is nil, it clears any previous error for the same bundle key.
248	// If newChecksum is non-empty, the bundle was successfully updated and the update is recorded.
249	// If err is non-nil, it stores the error for this bundle key.
250	// This method is called by the internal status callback.
251	func (m *pollerManager) recordPollResult(
252		policyNsName types.NamespacedName,
253		bundleKey graph.WAFBundleKey,
254		bundleDescription string,
255		newChecksum string,
256		err error,
257	) {
258		m.mu.Lock()
259		defer m.mu.Unlock()
260	
261		if err == nil {
262			if existing := m.pollErrors[policyNsName]; existing != nil && existing.BundleKey == bundleKey {
263				delete(m.pollErrors, policyNsName)
264			}
265			if newChecksum != "" {
266				m.bundleUpdates[policyNsName] = BundleUpdate{
267					BundleKey:         bundleKey,
268					BundleDescription: bundleDescription,
269					Checksum:          newChecksum,
270					UpdatedAt:         metav1.Now(),
271				}
272			}
273		} else {
274			m.pollErrors[policyNsName] = &PollError{
275				BundleKey:         bundleKey,
276				BundleDescription: bundleDescription,
277				Err:               err,
278			}
279		}
280	}
281	
282	// GetAllBundleUpdates returns a copy of the most recent successful bundle update per policy.
283	func (m *pollerManager) GetAllBundleUpdates() map[types.NamespacedName]BundleUpdate {
284		m.mu.RLock()
285		defer m.mu.RUnlock()
286	
287		if len(m.bundleUpdates) == 0 {
288			return nil
289		}
290	
291		result := make(map[types.NamespacedName]BundleUpdate, len(m.bundleUpdates))
292		maps.Copy(result, m.bundleUpdates)
293		return result
294	}
295	
296	// cacheBundleUpdate stores the latest successfully polled bundle data in the manager's cache.
297	// This is called by pollers when they detect a changed bundle, ensuring the freshest data
298	// is available for graph rebuild stale-bundle fallback.
299	// On the first time a bundle key appears in this manager's cache, a WAFBundleReconcileEvent
300	// is injected into the event loop to trigger an immediate graph rebuild.
301	// Note: this fires on any first-cache event, including after a poller restart that cleared the
302	// cache — not only when the policy was previously in BundlePending state. A spurious reconcile
303	// event in that case is harmless: it triggers an unnecessary graph rebuild but causes no
304	// incorrect behavior.
305	func (m *pollerManager) cacheBundleUpdate(bundleKey graph.WAFBundleKey, data []byte, checksum string) {
306		m.mu.Lock()
307	
308		_, alreadyCached := m.bundleCache[bundleKey]
309	
310		m.bundleCache[bundleKey] = &graph.WAFBundleData{
311			Data:     data,
312			Checksum: checksum,
313		}
314	
315		// Capture event details while holding the lock, then release before sending.
316		var event *events.WAFBundleReconcileEvent
317		if !alreadyCached {
318			if policyNsName, ok := m.bundleKeyToPolicy[bundleKey]; ok && m.eventCh != nil {
319				event = &events.WAFBundleReconcileEvent{PolicyNsName: policyNsName}
320			}
321		}
322	
323		m.mu.Unlock()
324	
325		// Send the reconcile event after releasing the lock so other manager operations are not
326		// blocked on the mutex while waiting for the event loop. The manager's root context is
327		// used as a cancellation escape hatch: on shutdown, the event loop exits before the
328		// manager's context is canceled, so without this the poller goroutine could block
329		// indefinitely trying to send to an already-drained channel.
330		if event != nil {
331			select {
332			case m.eventCh <- *event:
333			case <-m.ctx.Done():
334			}
335		}
336	}
337	
338	// GetAllPollErrors returns a deep copy of all current poll errors.
339	func (m *pollerManager) GetAllPollErrors() map[types.NamespacedName]PollError {
340		m.mu.RLock()
341		defer m.mu.RUnlock()
342	
343		if len(m.pollErrors) == 0 {
344			return nil
345		}
346	
347		result := make(map[types.NamespacedName]PollError, len(m.pollErrors))
348		for k, v := range m.pollErrors {
349			result[k] = *v
350		}
351		return result
352	}
353	
354	// GetLatestBundles returns a copy of all bundles that have been successfully fetched by pollers.
355	// These represent the freshest known bundle data and should take precedence over
356	// graph-cached bundles when constructing stale-bundle fallback state.
357	func (m *pollerManager) GetLatestBundles() map[graph.WAFBundleKey]*graph.WAFBundleData {
358		m.mu.RLock()
359		defer m.mu.RUnlock()
360	
361		if len(m.bundleCache) == 0 {
362			return nil
363		}
364	
365		result := make(map[graph.WAFBundleKey]*graph.WAFBundleData, len(m.bundleCache))
366		maps.Copy(result, m.bundleCache)
367		return result
368	}
369	
370	// StopPoller stops the poller for a WAFPolicy.
371	func (m *pollerManager) StopPoller(policyNsName types.NamespacedName) {
372		m.mu.Lock()
373		entry, exists := m.pollers[policyNsName]
374		if !exists {
375			m.mu.Unlock()
376			return
377		}
378		delete(m.pollers, policyNsName)
379		delete(m.pollErrors, policyNsName)
380		delete(m.bundleUpdates, policyNsName)
381		m.clearBundleCacheLocked(entry.poller)
382		m.mu.Unlock()
383	
384		entry.cancel()
385		m.logger.Info("Stopped WAF poller", "policy", policyNsName)
386	}
387	
388	// stopAll stops all running pollers. Should be called during shutdown.
389	func (m *pollerManager) stopAll() {
390		m.mu.Lock()
391		entries := make([]*pollerEntry, 0, len(m.pollers))
392		for _, entry := range m.pollers {
393			entries = append(entries, entry)
394		}
395		m.pollers = make(map[types.NamespacedName]*pollerEntry)
396		m.pollErrors = make(map[types.NamespacedName]*PollError)
397		m.bundleUpdates = make(map[types.NamespacedName]BundleUpdate)
398		m.bundleCache = make(map[graph.WAFBundleKey]*graph.WAFBundleData)
399		m.bundleKeyToPolicy = make(map[graph.WAFBundleKey]types.NamespacedName)
400		m.bundleKeyToDescription = make(map[graph.WAFBundleKey]string)
401		m.mu.Unlock()
402	
403		for _, entry := range entries {
404			entry.cancel()
405		}
406	
407		m.logger.Info("Stopped all WAF pollers", "count", len(entries))
408	}
409	
410	// clearBundleCacheLocked removes cached bundle data and all per-key mappings for all bundle keys
411	// owned by the given poller. Must be called while m.mu is held.
412	func (m *pollerManager) clearBundleCacheLocked(p *poller) {
413		for _, src := range p.getSources() {
414			delete(m.bundleCache, src.BundleKey)
415			delete(m.bundleKeyToPolicy, src.BundleKey)
416			delete(m.bundleKeyToDescription, src.BundleKey)
417		}
418	}
419	
420	// HasPoller reports whether a poller is currently registered for the given policy.
421	func (m *pollerManager) HasPoller(policyNsName types.NamespacedName) bool {
422		m.mu.RLock()
423		defer m.mu.RUnlock()
424		_, exists := m.pollers[policyNsName]
425		return exists
426	}
427	
428	// StopPollersNotIn stops all pollers whose policy namespace/name is not in the given set.
429	// This is used to clean up pollers for policies that have been deleted or no longer need polling.
430	func (m *pollerManager) StopPollersNotIn(activePolicies map[types.NamespacedName]struct{}) {
431		m.mu.Lock()
432		var toStop []types.NamespacedName
433		for nsName := range m.pollers {
434			if _, active := activePolicies[nsName]; !active {
435				toStop = append(toStop, nsName)
436			}
437		}
438	
439		entriesToCancel := make([]*pollerEntry, 0, len(toStop))
440		for _, nsName := range toStop {
441			if entry, exists := m.pollers[nsName]; exists {
442				entriesToCancel = append(entriesToCancel, entry)
443				delete(m.pollers, nsName)
444				delete(m.pollErrors, nsName)
445				delete(m.bundleUpdates, nsName)
446				m.clearBundleCacheLocked(entry.poller)
447			}
448		}
449		m.mu.Unlock()
450	
451		for _, entry := range entriesToCancel {
452			entry.cancel()
453		}
454	
455		if len(toStop) > 0 {
456			m.logger.Info("Stopped stale WAF pollers", "count", len(toStop))
457		}
458	}

1	package poller
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"reflect"
7		"sync"
8		"time"
9	
10		"github.com/go-logr/logr"
11		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types"
12	
13		ngfAPIv1alpha1 "github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/apis/v1alpha1"
14		"github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/controller/nginx/agent"
15		"github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/controller/nginx/config"
16		"github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/controller/state/dataplane"
17		"github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/controller/state/graph"
18		"github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/framework/waf/fetch"
19	)
20	
21	// defaultPollingInterval is the default interval between poll cycles.
22	const defaultPollingInterval = 5 * time.Minute
23	
24	type BundleType int
25	
26	const (
27		PolicyBundle BundleType = iota
28		LogProfileBundle
29	)
30	
31	// BundleSource represents a single bundle source that needs polling.
32	// This can be either the main policy bundle or a log bundle.
33	type BundleSource struct {
34		// BundleKey is the unique identifier for this bundle.
35		BundleKey graph.WAFBundleKey
36		// Description is a human-readable label for this bundle, used in status conditions and log messages.
37		Description string
38		// Request contains the fetch configuration for this bundle.
39		Request fetch.Request
40		// Type indicates whether this is a policy bundle or a log profile bundle, which determines the fetch method used.
41		Type BundleType
42		// Interval is the polling interval for this source.
43		Interval time.Duration
44	}
45	
46	// bundleState tracks the last known state of a fetched bundle, including the checksum and any
47	// conditional-request validators (ETag or Last-Modified) for use on subsequent HTTP polls.
48	type bundleState struct {
49		checksum     string
50		eTag         string
51		lastModified string
52	}
53	
54	// poller handles periodic re-fetching of WAF bundles for a single WAFPolicy.
55	// It compares checksums to detect changes and pushes updated bundles to relevant deployments.
56	type poller struct {
57		fetcher           fetch.Fetcher
58		deployments       agent.DeploymentStorer
59		targetDeployments map[types.NamespacedName]struct{}
60		bundleStates      map[graph.WAFBundleKey]bundleState
61		statusCallback    func(
62			policyNsName types.NamespacedName,
63			bundleKey graph.WAFBundleKey,
64			newChecksum string,
65			err error,
66		)
67		bundleUpdateCallback func(bundleKey graph.WAFBundleKey, data []byte, checksum string)
68		policyNsName         types.NamespacedName
69		logger               logr.Logger
70		sources              []BundleSource
71		targetMu             sync.RWMutex
72		stateMu              sync.RWMutex
73	}
74	
75	// pollerConfig contains the configuration for creating a new poller.
76	type pollerConfig struct {
77		fetcher          fetch.Fetcher
78		deployments      agent.DeploymentStorer
79		initialChecksums map[graph.WAFBundleKey]string
80		statusCallback   func(
81			policyNsName types.NamespacedName,
82			bundleKey graph.WAFBundleKey,
83			newChecksum string,
84			err error,
85		)
86		bundleUpdateCallback func(bundleKey graph.WAFBundleKey, data []byte, checksum string)
87		policyNsName         types.NamespacedName
88		logger               logr.Logger
89		sources              []BundleSource
90		targetDeployments    []types.NamespacedName
91	}
92	
93	// newPoller creates a new poller for the given WAFPolicy.
94	func newPoller(cfg pollerConfig) *poller {
95		targets := make(map[types.NamespacedName]struct{}, len(cfg.targetDeployments))
96		for _, t := range cfg.targetDeployments {
97			targets[t] = struct{}{}
98		}
99	
100		states := make(map[graph.WAFBundleKey]bundleState, len(cfg.initialChecksums))
101		for k, cs := range cfg.initialChecksums {
102			states[k] = bundleState{checksum: cs}
103		}
104	
105		return &poller{
106			logger:               cfg.logger.WithValues("policy", cfg.policyNsName),
107			policyNsName:         cfg.policyNsName,
108			sources:              cfg.sources,
109			fetcher:              cfg.fetcher,
110			deployments:          cfg.deployments,
111			targetDeployments:    targets,
112			bundleStates:         states,
113			statusCallback:       cfg.statusCallback,
114			bundleUpdateCallback: cfg.bundleUpdateCallback,
115		}
116	}
117	
118	// run starts the polling loop. It blocks until the context is canceled.
119	func (p *poller) run(ctx context.Context) {
120		if len(p.sources) == 0 {
121			p.logger.V(1).Info("No sources with polling enabled, poller exiting")
122			return
123		}
124	
125		// Find the minimum interval among all sources to use as the tick interval.
126		minInterval := p.sources[0].Interval
127		for _, src := range p.sources[1:] {
128			minInterval = min(minInterval, src.Interval)
129		}
130	
131		if minInterval <= 0 {
132			p.logger.Error(nil, fmt.Sprintf(
133				"Invalid polling interval, must be greater than zero. Using default interval: %v", defaultPollingInterval,
134			))
135			minInterval = defaultPollingInterval
136		}
137	
138		p.logger.Info("WAF polling started", "interval", minInterval, "sourceCount", len(p.sources))
139	
140		// Track last poll time for each source to handle different intervals.
141		lastPoll := make(map[graph.WAFBundleKey]time.Time, len(p.sources))
142	
143		// Poll all sources immediately on startup, then start the ticker for subsequent polls.
144		now := time.Now()
145		for _, src := range p.sources {
146			p.pollSource(ctx, src)
147			lastPoll[src.BundleKey] = now
148		}
149	
150		ticker := time.NewTicker(minInterval)
151		defer ticker.Stop()
152	
153		for {
154			select {
155			case <-ctx.Done():
156				p.logger.V(1).Info("Poller stopping due to context cancellation")
157				return
158			case now := <-ticker.C:
159				for _, src := range p.sources {
160					if now.Sub(lastPoll[src.BundleKey]) >= src.Interval {
161						p.pollSource(ctx, src)
162						lastPoll[src.BundleKey] = now
163					}
164				}
165			}
166		}
167	}
168	
169	// getTargetDeployments returns the current set of target deployment names.
170	func (p *poller) getTargetDeployments() []types.NamespacedName {
171		p.targetMu.RLock()
172		defer p.targetMu.RUnlock()
173	
174		targets := make([]types.NamespacedName, 0, len(p.targetDeployments))
175		for t := range p.targetDeployments {
176			targets = append(targets, t)
177		}
178	
179		return targets
180	}
181	
182	// updateTargetDeployments updates the set of deployments this poller should push bundles to.
183	func (p *poller) updateTargetDeployments(targets []types.NamespacedName) {
184		newTargets := make(map[types.NamespacedName]struct{}, len(targets))
185		for _, t := range targets {
186			newTargets[t] = struct{}{}
187		}
188	
189		p.targetMu.Lock()
190		defer p.targetMu.Unlock()
191	
192		p.targetDeployments = newTargets
193	}
194	
195	// getSources returns the bundle sources this poller is monitoring.
196	func (p *poller) getSources() []BundleSource {
197		return p.sources
198	}
199	
200	// sourcesEqual returns true if two BundleSource slices are equivalent.
201	// This is used to determine if a poller needs to be restarted due to source changes.
202	func sourcesEqual(a, b []BundleSource) bool {
203		return reflect.DeepEqual(a, b)
204	}
205	
206	// pollSource fetches a single bundle source and pushes it to deployments if changed.
207	//
208	// For NIM and N1C sources a two-phase approach is used: first only the remote checksum is
209	// retrieved, and the full bundle is downloaded only when the checksum differs from the last
210	// known value.
211	//
212	// For plain HTTP sources a conditional GET is issued using any ETag or Last-Modified token
213	// stored from the previous successful fetch. A 304 Not Modified response is treated as
214	// unchanged without downloading the bundle.
215	func (p *poller) pollSource(ctx context.Context, src BundleSource) {
216		p.logger.V(1).Info("Polling bundle source", "bundle", src.BundleKey)
217	
218		p.stateMu.RLock()
219		last := p.bundleStates[src.BundleKey]
220		p.stateMu.RUnlock()
221	
222		if src.Request.SupportsChecksumOnlyFetch() {
223			if skip := p.skipIfChecksumUnchanged(ctx, src, last.checksum); skip {
224				return
225			}
226		}
227	
228		result, err := p.downloadBundle(ctx, src, last)
229		if err != nil {
230			p.reportStatus(src.BundleKey, "", err)
231			return
232		}
233	
234		if result.Unchanged {
235			// 304 Not Modified: content is the same. Preserve the existing checksum and persist
236			// any rotated ETag/Last-Modified the server sent back so future polls can use it.
237			result.Checksum = last.checksum
238			p.saveBundleState(src.BundleKey, result)
239			p.reportStatus(src.BundleKey, "", nil)
240			return
241		}
242	
243		if result.Checksum == last.checksum {
244			// Content is identical but the server may have rotated its conditional token (ETag /
245			// Last-Modified). Save the new token so the next poll can use it, avoiding a full
246			// re-download every interval when the validator changes while content stays the same.
247			p.saveBundleState(src.BundleKey, result)
248			p.reportStatus(src.BundleKey, "", nil)
249			return
250		}
251	
252		p.logger.Info("Bundle changed, pushing to deployments", "bundle", src.BundleKey, "newChecksum", result.Checksum)
253		p.pushBundleToDeployments(src.BundleKey, result.Data)
254		p.saveBundleState(src.BundleKey, result)
255	
256		if p.bundleUpdateCallback != nil {
257			p.bundleUpdateCallback(src.BundleKey, result.Data, result.Checksum)
258		}
259		p.reportStatus(src.BundleKey, result.Checksum, nil)
260	}
261	
262	// skipIfChecksumUnchanged fetches only the remote checksum for a NIM or N1C source.
263	// It reports whether polling should skip the full download (true = skip). When skipping due to
264	// an error or an unchanged checksum the appropriate status callback is fired.
265	func (p *poller) skipIfChecksumUnchanged(ctx context.Context, src BundleSource, lastChecksum string) bool {
266		changed, checksum, err := p.checksumChanged(ctx, src, lastChecksum)
267		if err != nil {
268			p.logger.Error(err, "Failed to fetch bundle checksum during poll", "bundle", src.BundleKey)
269			p.reportStatus(src.BundleKey, "", err)
270			return true
271		}
272		if !changed {
273			p.logger.V(1).Info("Bundle unchanged, skipping download", "bundle", src.BundleKey)
274			p.reportStatus(src.BundleKey, "", nil)
275			return true
276		}
277		p.logger.Info("Bundle checksum changed, downloading full bundle", "bundle", src.BundleKey, "newChecksum", checksum)
278		return false
279	}
280	
281	// downloadBundle fetches the full bundle for src, attaching the stored conditional token so that
282	// HTTP servers can respond with 304 Not Modified when nothing has changed.
283	func (p *poller) downloadBundle(
284		ctx context.Context, src BundleSource, last bundleState,
285	) (fetch.Result, error) {
286		req := src.Request
287		req.ETag = last.eTag
288		req.LastModified = last.lastModified
289	
290		result, err := p.fetchBundle(ctx, src, req)
291		if err != nil {
292			p.logger.Error(err, "Failed to fetch bundle during poll", "bundle", src.BundleKey)
293			return fetch.Result{}, err
294		}
295		if result.Unchanged {
296			p.logger.V(1).Info("Bundle unchanged (304), skipping push", "bundle", src.BundleKey)
297		} else if result.Checksum == last.checksum {
298			p.logger.V(1).Info("Bundle unchanged, skipping push", "bundle", src.BundleKey)
299		}
300		return result, nil
301	}
302	
303	// saveBundleState persists the checksum and HTTP conditional token after a successful fetch,
304	// including when no push occurs because the content is unchanged but the conditional token rotated.
305	// A previously stored conditional token is preserved when the response does not supply a new one,
306	// so that servers omitting validators on some responses do not force unconditional GETs.
307	func (p *poller) saveBundleState(bundleKey graph.WAFBundleKey, result fetch.Result) {
308		p.stateMu.Lock()
309		defer p.stateMu.Unlock()
310	
311		state := p.bundleStates[bundleKey]
312		state.checksum = result.Checksum
313		if result.ETag != "" {
314			state.eTag = result.ETag
315		}
316		if result.LastModified != "" {
317			state.lastModified = result.LastModified
318		}
319		// If ETag or Last-Modified is absent in the response, the previously stored value is preserved
320		// so that servers omitting validators on some responses do not force unconditional GETs.
321		p.bundleStates[bundleKey] = state
322	}
323	
324	// reportStatus fires the status callback if one is registered.
325	// newChecksum is non-empty when the bundle was successfully updated; empty for unchanged or error cases.
326	func (p *poller) reportStatus(bundleKey graph.WAFBundleKey, newChecksum string, err error) {
327		if p.statusCallback != nil {
328			p.statusCallback(p.policyNsName, bundleKey, newChecksum, err)
329		}
330	}
331	
332	// checksumChanged fetches only the remote checksum for a NIM or N1C source and reports whether
333	// it differs from lastChecksum. Returns (changed, remoteChecksum, error).
334	func (p *poller) checksumChanged(ctx context.Context, src BundleSource, lastChecksum string) (bool, string, error) {
335		var checksum string
336		var err error
337	
338		if src.Type == LogProfileBundle {
339			checksum, err = p.fetcher.FetchLogProfileBundleChecksum(ctx, src.Request)
340		} else {
341			checksum, err = p.fetcher.FetchPolicyBundleChecksum(ctx, src.Request)
342		}
343		if err != nil {
344			return false, "", err
345		}
346	
347		return checksum != lastChecksum, checksum, nil
348	}
349	
350	// fetchBundle downloads the full bundle for the given source using req (which may carry a
351	// conditional token for HTTP sources).
352	func (p *poller) fetchBundle(ctx context.Context, src BundleSource, req fetch.Request) (fetch.Result, error) {
353		if src.Type == LogProfileBundle {
354			return p.fetcher.FetchLogProfileBundle(ctx, req)
355		}
356		return p.fetcher.FetchPolicyBundle(ctx, req)
357	}
358	
359	// pushBundleToDeployments pushes the bundle to all target deployments.
360	func (p *poller) pushBundleToDeployments(bundleKey graph.WAFBundleKey, data []byte) {
361		p.targetMu.RLock()
362		defer p.targetMu.RUnlock()
363	
364		bundlePath := config.GenerateWAFBundleFileName(dataplane.WAFBundleID(bundleKey))
365	
366		for depName := range p.targetDeployments {
367			deployment := p.deployments.Get(depName)
368			if deployment == nil {
369				p.logger.V(1).Info("Deployment not found, skipping bundle push", "deployment", depName)
370				continue
371			}
372	
373			deployment.FileLock.Lock()
374			msg := deployment.UpdateWAFBundle(bundlePath, data)
375			if msg != nil {
376				applied := deployment.GetBroadcaster().Send(*msg)
377				if applied {
378					p.logger.Info(
379						"Pushed updated WAF bundle to deployment",
380						"deployment", depName,
381					)
382				} else {
383					p.logger.V(1).Info(
384						"No subscribers for deployment, bundle stored but not pushed",
385						"deployment", depName,
386					)
387				}
388			}
389			deployment.FileLock.Unlock()
390		}
391	}
392	
393	// BuildBundleSources constructs BundleSource entries from a WAFPolicy spec.
394	// It returns only sources that have polling enabled.
395	func BuildBundleSources(
396		policyNsName types.NamespacedName,
397		spec ngfAPIv1alpha1.WAFPolicySpec,
398		auth *fetch.BundleAuth,
399		tlsCA []byte,
400	) []BundleSource {
401		var sources []BundleSource
402	
403		// Check if policySource has polling enabled.
404		if spec.PolicySource != nil && spec.PolicySource.Polling != nil && spec.PolicySource.Polling.Enabled {
405			interval := defaultPollingInterval
406			if spec.PolicySource.Polling.Interval != nil && spec.PolicySource.Polling.Interval.Duration > 0 {
407				interval = spec.PolicySource.Polling.Interval.Duration
408			}
409	
410			sources = append(sources, BundleSource{
411				Type:        PolicyBundle,
412				BundleKey:   graph.PolicyBundleKey(policyNsName),
413				Request:     graph.BuildPolicyFetchRequest(spec.PolicySource, spec.Type, auth, tlsCA),
414				Description: "policy bundle",
415				Interval:    interval,
416			})
417		}
418	
419		// Check each logSource for polling.
420		for _, secLog := range spec.SecurityLogs {
421			if secLog.LogSource == nil {
422				continue
423			}
424			if secLog.LogSource.HTTPSource == nil && secLog.LogSource.NIMSource == nil && secLog.LogSource.N1CSource == nil {
425				continue // DefaultProfile, no polling needed.
426			}
427			if secLog.LogSource.Polling == nil || !secLog.LogSource.Polling.Enabled {
428				continue
429			}
430	
431			interval := defaultPollingInterval
432			if secLog.LogSource.Polling.Interval != nil && secLog.LogSource.Polling.Interval.Duration > 0 {
433				interval = secLog.LogSource.Polling.Interval.Duration
434			}
435	
436			sources = append(sources, BundleSource{
437				Type:        LogProfileBundle,
438				BundleKey:   graph.LogBundleKey(policyNsName, secLog.LogSource),
439				Request:     graph.BuildLogFetchRequest(secLog.LogSource, auth, tlsCA),
440				Description: graph.LogBundleDescription(secLog.LogSource),
441				Interval:    interval,
442			})
443		}
444	
445		return sources
446	}

1	package fetch
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"crypto/sha256"
6		"crypto/tls"
7		"crypto/x509"
8		"encoding/base64"
9		"encoding/hex"
10		"encoding/json"
11		"errors"
12		"fmt"
13		"io"
14		"net/http"
15		"net/url"
16		"slices"
17		"strings"
18		"time"
19	
20		"github.com/go-logr/logr"
21		metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
22		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait"
23	
24		"github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/framework/waf"
25	)
26	
27	//go:generate go tool counterfeiter -generate
28	
29	const (
30		defaultTimeout = 30 * time.Second
31		// n1cCompileStatusPollInterval is the interval between polls when waiting for an N1C
32		// async compilation job to complete.
33		n1cCompileStatusPollInterval = 10 * time.Second
34	
35		// retryBaseDelay is the initial delay for retrying fetches on transient failures.
36		retryBaseDelay = 1 * time.Second
37		// retryMaxDelay is the maximum delay for retrying fetches on transient failures.
38		retryMaxDelay = 30 * time.Second
39	
40		// headerIfNoneMatch and headerIfModifiedSince are the conditional-GET request headers.
41		headerIfNoneMatch     = "If-None-Match"
42		headerIfModifiedSince = "If-Modified-Since"
43		// headerETag and headerLastModified are the validator response headers.
44		headerETag         = "ETag"
45		headerLastModified = "Last-Modified"
46	)
47	
48	// unixEpochRFC3339 is the Unix epoch formatted as RFC3339, used as a startTime sentinel
49	// to retrieve all policies regardless of age.
50	var unixEpochRFC3339 = time.Unix(0, 0).UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
51	
52	type NAPCompilerVersionResponse struct {
53		Version string `json:"version"`
54	}
55	
56	type LogProfileBundleResponse struct {
57		CompiledBundle string             `json:"compiledBundle"`
58		MetaData       LogProfileMetaData `json:"metaData"`
59	}
60	
61	type LogProfileMetaData struct {
62		CompilerVersion string `json:"compilerVersion"`
63		Created         string `json:"created"`
64		LogProfileName  string `json:"logProfileName"`
65		LogProfileUID   string `json:"logProfileUid"`
66		Modified        string `json:"modified"`
67		UID             string `json:"uid"`
68	}
69	
70	// Result holds the outcome of a bundle fetch operation.
71	type Result struct {
72		Checksum     string
73		ETag         string
74		LastModified string
75		Data         []byte
76		Unchanged    bool
77	}
78	
79	// BundleAuth holds authentication credentials for bundle fetching.
80	type BundleAuth struct {
81		Username    string
82		Password    string
83		BearerToken string
84		// APIToken is used for F5 NGINX One Console (N1C) requests.
85		// Sent as "Authorization: APIToken <token>" rather than the Bearer scheme.
86		APIToken string
87	}
88	
89	// Request carries all parameters needed to fetch a single bundle.
90	type Request struct {
91		Auth    *BundleAuth
92		Timeout *metav1.Duration
93		// N1C holds the N1C specific request details.
94		N1C N1CRequest
95		// LogProfileName is the human-readable name of the log profile.
96		// Used to look up the log profile by name when fetching from NIM or N1C.
97		// Mutually exclusive with N1C.LogProfileObjectID.
98		LogProfileName string
99		// PolicyName is the human-readable name of the policy.
100		// Used to look up the policy by name when fetching from NIM or N1C.
101		// Mutually exclusive with NIM.PolicyUID or N1C.PolicyObjectID.
102		PolicyName string
103		// NIM holds the NIM specific request details.
104		NIM NIMRequest
105		// URL is the base URL of the bundle source.
106		URL string
107		// ExpectedChecksum is the hex-encoded SHA-256 checksum the downloaded bundle must match.
108		// When set, NGF verifies the bundle after download and rejects it if the checksum differs.
109		// Mutually exclusive with VerifyChecksum. Supported for all source types.
110		ExpectedChecksum string
111		// ETag is the value of the ETag response header from the previous successful HTTP fetch.
112		// When set, it is sent as If-None-Match on the next request so the server can respond
113		// with 304 Not Modified instead of retransmitting the bundle. Takes precedence over LastModified.
114		// Only used for plain HTTP sources; ignored for NIM and N1C.
115		ETag string
116		// LastModified is the value of the Last-Modified response header from the previous successful
117		// HTTP fetch. When set, it is sent as If-Modified-Since on the next request. Only used when
118		// ETag is empty, since ETag is the preferred validator. Only used for plain HTTP sources; ignored
119		// for NIM and N1C.
120		LastModified string
121		// TLSCAData is the PEM-encoded CA certificate used to verify the bundle server's TLS certificate.
122		// When nil, the system certificate pool is used.
123		TLSCAData []byte
124		// RetryAttempts is the maximum number of additional retries for transient errors
125		// (network failures, HTTP 5xx responses). Non-transient errors (HTTP 4xx,
126		// checksum mismatch) are never retried. When zero, the request is
127		// attempted exactly once with no retries.
128		RetryAttempts int32
129		// InsecureSkipVerify disables TLS certificate verification. Not recommended for production use.
130		InsecureSkipVerify bool
131		// VerifyChecksum enables checksum verification by fetching a companion <url>.sha256 file.
132		// Only supported for plain HTTP fetches; not supported for NIM or N1C policy or log-profile
133		// sources (PolicyName, NIM.PolicyUID, N1C.Namespace, or LogProfileName set).
134		// Mutually exclusive with ExpectedChecksum.
135		VerifyChecksum bool
136	}
137	
138	// N1CRequest carries all the N1C specific parameters to fetch a single bundle.
139	type N1CRequest struct {
140		// Namespace is the F5 NGINX One Console namespace the policy belongs to.
141		// Required when fetching from N1C (i.e. when Namespace is non-empty).
142		Namespace string
143		// PolicyObjectID is the opaque N1C policy object ID (e.g. "pol_-IUuEUN7ST63oRC7AlQPLw").
144		// When set, the policies list call is skipped and this ID is used directly.
145		PolicyObjectID string
146		// PolicyVersionID pins the fetch to a specific version using its opaque version ID
147		// (e.g. "pv_1234"). When empty, the latest version is used.
148		PolicyVersionID string
149		// LogProfileObjectID is the opaque N1C log profile object ID (e.g. "lp_8s8uZxLpThWwEGF7LTn_rA").
150		// When set, the log profiles list call is skipped and this ID is used directly.
151		LogProfileObjectID string
152	}
153	
154	// NIMRequest carries all the NIM specific parameters to fetch a single bundle.
155	type NIMRequest struct {
156		// PolicyUID is the unique identifier of a specific version of the NIM policy
157		// (e.g. "2bc1e3ac-7990-4ca4-910a-8634c444c804"). Each policy version has a distinct UID,
158		// so setting this field pins the fetch to that exact version.
159		// Mutually exclusive with PolicyName.
160		PolicyUID string
161	}
162	
163	// Fetcher fetches WAF policy bundles and log profile bundles from remote sources.
164	//
165	//counterfeiter:generate . Fetcher
166	type Fetcher interface {
167		// FetchPolicyBundle retrieves the policy bundle described by req.
168		// For HTTP sources: GETs req.URL; sends If-None-Match when req.ETag is set, or If-Modified-Since
169		// when req.LastModified is set;
170		// and returns Result.Unchanged=true on 304. If req.VerifyChecksum is true, fetches
171		// req.URL+".sha256" to verify integrity. VerifyChecksum is not supported for NIM/N1C.
172		// For NIM sources: calls the NIM bundles API and base64-decodes items[0].content.
173		// For N1C sources: resolves the policy via the N1C API and downloads the compiled bundle.
174		FetchPolicyBundle(ctx context.Context, req Request) (Result, error)
175		// FetchLogProfileBundle retrieves the log profile bundle described by req.
176		// For HTTP sources: same conditional-request behavior as FetchPolicyBundle.
177		// For NIM sources: calls the NIM log profile bundles API and base64-decodes the compiledBundle field.
178		// For N1C sources: resolves the log profile via the N1C API and downloads the compiled bundle.
179		FetchLogProfileBundle(ctx context.Context, req Request) (Result, error)
180		// FetchPolicyBundleChecksum retrieves only the checksum of the remote policy bundle without
181		// downloading the full bundle content. Only supported for NIM and N1C sources; returns an error
182		// for plain HTTP sources (use FetchPolicyBundle with ETag/LastModified instead).
183		FetchPolicyBundleChecksum(ctx context.Context, req Request) (checksum string, err error)
184		// FetchLogProfileBundleChecksum retrieves only the checksum of the remote log profile bundle without
185		// downloading the full bundle content. Only supported for NIM and N1C sources; returns an error
186		// for plain HTTP sources (use FetchLogProfileBundle with ConditionalToken instead).
187		FetchLogProfileBundleChecksum(ctx context.Context, req Request) (checksum string, err error)
188	}
189	
190	// HTTPFetcher implements Fetcher using HTTP/HTTPS.
191	// It keeps a default client for requests that need no custom TLS settings,
192	// and builds a short-lived client only when a per-request CA or insecure flag is set.
193	type HTTPFetcher struct {
194		defaultClient       *http.Client
195		logger              logr.Logger
196		n1cCompilePollDelay time.Duration
197	}
198	
199	// NewHTTPFetcher creates a new HTTPFetcher.
200	func NewHTTPFetcher(logger logr.Logger) *HTTPFetcher {
201		return &HTTPFetcher{
202			logger:              logger,
203			defaultClient:       &http.Client{},
204			n1cCompilePollDelay: n1cCompileStatusPollInterval,
205		}
206	}
207	
208	// WithN1CCompilePollDelay overrides the interval between N1C compile status polls.
209	// Intended for testing only.
210	func (f *HTTPFetcher) WithN1CCompilePollDelay(d time.Duration) *HTTPFetcher {
211		f.n1cCompilePollDelay = d
212		return f
213	}
214	
215	// validateAndNormalizeRequest checks mutual-exclusion rules and normalises
216	// ExpectedChecksum to lowercase. It returns the updated Request or an error.
217	func validateAndNormalizeRequest(req Request) (Request, error) {
218		if req.VerifyChecksum &&
219			(req.N1C.Namespace != "" || req.PolicyName != "" || req.NIM.PolicyUID != "" || req.LogProfileName != "") {
220			return Request{}, fmt.Errorf(
221				"verifyChecksum is only supported for plain HTTP fetches; use expectedChecksum for NIM/N1C sources",
222			)
223		}
224	
225		if req.ExpectedChecksum != "" {
226			normalized := strings.ToLower(req.ExpectedChecksum)
227			if _, err := hex.DecodeString(normalized); err != nil || len(normalized) != 64 {
228				return Request{}, fmt.Errorf(
229					"invalid expected checksum %q: must be 64 hex characters", req.ExpectedChecksum,
230				)
231			}
232			req.ExpectedChecksum = normalized
233		}
234	
235		return req, nil
236	}
237	
238	// nonTransientError wraps errors that must not be retried (HTTP 4xx, checksum mismatch, etc.).
239	type nonTransientError struct {
240		err error
241	}
242	
243	func (e *nonTransientError) Error() string { return e.err.Error() }
244	func (e *nonTransientError) Unwrap() error { return e.err }
245	
246	// FetchPolicyBundle retrieves policy bundle bytes.
247	// When req.N1C.Namespace is set, uses N1C fetch logic (APIToken auth, N1C API path).
248	// When req.PolicyName or req.NIM.PolicyUID is set (and N1C.Namespace is empty), uses NIM fetch logic.
249	// Otherwise performs a plain GET to req.URL, optionally verifying the checksum.
250	// For plain HTTP sources, a conditional GET is issued when req.ETag or req.LastModified is set.
251	func (f *HTTPFetcher) FetchPolicyBundle(ctx context.Context, req Request) (Result, error) {
252		return f.fetch(ctx, req, f.dispatch)
253	}
254	
255	// FetchLogProfileBundle retrieves log profile bundle bytes.
256	// When req.N1C.Namespace is set, uses N1C fetch logic (APIToken auth, N1C API path).
257	// When req.LogProfileName is set (and N1C.Namespace is empty), uses NIM fetch logic.
258	// Otherwise performs a plain GET to req.URL.
259	// For plain HTTP sources, a conditional GET is issued when req.ETag or req.LastModified is set.
260	func (f *HTTPFetcher) FetchLogProfileBundle(ctx context.Context, req Request) (Result, error) {
261		return f.fetch(ctx, req, f.logProfileDispatch)
262	}
263	
264	// FetchPolicyBundleChecksum returns only the checksum of the remote policy bundle for NIM and N1C
265	// sources without downloading the full bundle content. Returns an error for plain HTTP sources.
266	func (f *HTTPFetcher) FetchPolicyBundleChecksum(ctx context.Context, req Request) (string, error) {
267		result, err := f.fetch(ctx, req, f.dispatchChecksum)
268		return result.Checksum, err
269	}
270	
271	// FetchLogProfileBundleChecksum returns only the checksum of the remote log profile bundle for NIM
272	// and N1C sources without downloading the full bundle content. Returns an error for plain HTTP sources.
273	func (f *HTTPFetcher) FetchLogProfileBundleChecksum(ctx context.Context, req Request) (string, error) {
274		result, err := f.fetch(ctx, req, f.logProfileDispatchChecksum)
275		return result.Checksum, err
276	}
277	
278	func (f *HTTPFetcher) dispatchChecksum(
279		ctx context.Context,
280		client *http.Client,
281		req Request,
282	) (Result, error) {
283		switch {
284		case req.N1C.Namespace != "":
285			checksum, err := fetchN1CChecksum(ctx, client, req, f.n1cCompilePollDelay, f.logger)
286			return Result{Checksum: checksum}, err
287		case req.PolicyName != "" || req.NIM.PolicyUID != "":
288			checksum, err := fetchNIMChecksum(ctx, client, req, f.logger)
289			return Result{Checksum: checksum}, err
290		default:
291			return Result{}, &nonTransientError{
292				err: fmt.Errorf("FetchPolicyBundleChecksum is not supported for plain HTTP sources"),
293			}
294		}
295	}
296	
297	func (f *HTTPFetcher) logProfileDispatchChecksum(
298		ctx context.Context,
299		client *http.Client,
300		req Request,
301	) (Result, error) {
302		switch {
303		case req.N1C.Namespace != "":
304			checksum, err := fetchN1CLogProfileChecksum(ctx, client, req, f.n1cCompilePollDelay, f.logger)
305			return Result{Checksum: checksum}, err
306		case req.LogProfileName != "":
307			checksum, err := fetchNIMLogProfileChecksum(ctx, client, req)
308			return Result{Checksum: checksum}, err
309		default:
310			return Result{}, &nonTransientError{
311				err: fmt.Errorf("FetchLogProfileBundleChecksum is not supported for plain HTTP sources"),
312			}
313		}
314	}
315	
316	func (f *HTTPFetcher) fetch(
317		ctx context.Context,
318		req Request,
319		dispatch func(ctx context.Context, client *http.Client, req Request) (Result, error),
320	) (Result, error) {
321		var err error
322		if req, err = validateAndNormalizeRequest(req); err != nil {
323			return Result{}, err
324		}
325	
326		client, err := f.buildHTTPClient(req)
327		if err != nil {
328			return Result{}, err
329		}
330		// buildHTTPClient creates a custom transport when TLS or insecure settings are configured.
331		// Unlike the shared default transport, this transport is not managed globally, so we close
332		// its idle connections when Fetch returns to prevent connection leaks.
333		if len(req.TLSCAData) > 0 || req.InsecureSkipVerify {
334			defer client.CloseIdleConnections()
335		}
336	
337		// FIXME(ciarams87): The retry loop runs synchronously
338		// inside Process(), which blocks the event handler and delays all config pushes to NGINX until
339		// retries complete. We should investigate making this async as retry
340		// counts or server latency could grow large enough to cause meaningful delays.
341		backoff := newRetryBackoff(req.RetryAttempts)
342	
343		var (
344			result  Result
345			lastErr error
346		)
347		attempt := 0
348		err = wait.ExponentialBackoffWithContext(ctx, backoff, func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {
349			attempt++
350			fr, fetchErr := dispatch(ctx, client, req)
351			if fetchErr != nil {
352				var nte *nonTransientError
353				if errors.As(fetchErr, &nte) {
354					return false, fetchErr
355				}
356				lastErr = fetchErr
357				f.logger.V(1).Info("Transient fetch error, retrying",
358					"attempt", attempt, "maxAttempts", backoff.Steps, "error", fetchErr)
359				return false, nil
360			}
361			result = fr
362			return true, nil
363		})
364		if err != nil {
365			if wait.Interrupted(err) {
366				return Result{}, lastErr
367			}
368			return Result{}, err
369		}
370	
371		if !result.Unchanged && req.ExpectedChecksum != "" && result.Checksum != req.ExpectedChecksum {
372			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf(
373				"bundle checksum mismatch: expected %s, got %s", req.ExpectedChecksum, result.Checksum,
374			)
375		}
376	
377		return result, nil
378	}
379	
380	func (f *HTTPFetcher) dispatch(ctx context.Context, client *http.Client, req Request) (Result, error) {
381		switch {
382		case req.N1C.Namespace != "":
383			return fetchN1C(ctx, client, req, f.n1cCompilePollDelay, f.logger)
384		case req.PolicyName != "" || req.NIM.PolicyUID != "":
385			return fetchNIM(ctx, client, req, f.logger)
386		default:
387			return fetchHTTP(ctx, client, req)
388		}
389	}
390	
391	func (f *HTTPFetcher) logProfileDispatch(
392		ctx context.Context,
393		client *http.Client,
394		req Request,
395	) (Result, error) {
396		switch {
397		case req.N1C.Namespace != "":
398			return fetchN1CLogProfile(ctx, client, req)
399		case req.LogProfileName != "":
400			return fetchNIMLogProfile(ctx, client, req)
401		default:
402			return fetchHTTP(ctx, client, req)
403		}
404	}
405	
406	// newRetryBackoff returns a Backoff configured with the project-wide retry defaults.
407	// Steps is set to retryAttempts+1 so that the initial attempt is always made even
408	// when retryAttempts is zero.
409	func newRetryBackoff(retryAttempts int32) wait.Backoff {
410		return wait.Backoff{
411			Duration: retryBaseDelay,
412			Factor:   2.0,
413			Jitter:   1.0,
414			Cap:      retryMaxDelay,
415			Steps:    int(retryAttempts) + 1,
416		}
417	}
418	
419	// buildHTTPClient returns an *http.Client configured for the given request.
420	func (f *HTTPFetcher) buildHTTPClient(req Request) (*http.Client, error) {
421		timeout := defaultTimeout
422		if req.Timeout != nil {
423			timeout = req.Timeout.Duration
424		}
425	
426		if len(req.TLSCAData) == 0 && !req.InsecureSkipVerify {
427			c := *f.defaultClient
428			c.Timeout = timeout
429			return &c, nil
430		}
431	
432		client, err := buildClient(req.TLSCAData, req.InsecureSkipVerify, timeout)
433		if err != nil {
434			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to build HTTP client: %w", err)
435		}
436		return client, nil
437	}
438	
439	// buildClient returns an *http.Client with the given timeout. When caData is non-nil, the CA is
440	// appended to the system cert pool. When insecureSkipVerify is true, TLS verification is disabled.
441	func buildClient(caData []byte, insecureSkipVerify bool, timeout time.Duration) (*http.Client, error) {
442		if caData == nil && !insecureSkipVerify {
443			return &http.Client{Timeout: timeout}, nil
444		}
445	
446		transport, ok := http.DefaultTransport.(*http.Transport)
447		if !ok {
448			return nil, fmt.Errorf("http.DefaultTransport is not *http.Transport")
449		}
450		transport = transport.Clone()
451		transport.TLSClientConfig = &tls.Config{
452			MinVersion:         tls.VersionTLS12,
453			InsecureSkipVerify: insecureSkipVerify, //nolint:gosec // intentional; documented as testing-only
454		}
455	
456		if caData != nil {
457			pool, err := x509.SystemCertPool()
458			if err != nil {
459				pool = x509.NewCertPool()
460			}
461			if !pool.AppendCertsFromPEM(caData) {
462				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to append CA certificate to pool")
463			}
464			transport.TLSClientConfig.RootCAs = pool
465		}
466	
467		return &http.Client{
468			Timeout:   timeout,
469			Transport: transport,
470		}, nil
471	}
472	
473	// fetchHTTP performs a GET to req.URL.
474	// When req.ETag is set it is sent as If-None-Match; when req.LastModified is set (and ETag is empty)
475	// it is sent as If-Modified-Since. A 304 response is returned as Result{Unchanged: true}.
476	// If req.VerifyChecksum is true, fetches req.URL+".sha256" and verifies integrity.
477	func fetchHTTP(ctx context.Context, client *http.Client, req Request) (Result, error) {
478		var extraHeaders map[string]string
479		switch {
480		case req.ETag != "":
481			extraHeaders = map[string]string{headerIfNoneMatch: req.ETag}
482		case req.LastModified != "":
483			extraHeaders = map[string]string{headerIfModifiedSince: req.LastModified}
484		}
485	
486		data, respHeaders, statusCode, err := doGetWithHeaders(ctx, client, req.URL, req.Auth, extraHeaders,
487			http.StatusOK, http.StatusNotModified)
488		if err != nil {
489			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to fetch bundle: %w", err)
490		}
491	
492		if statusCode == http.StatusNotModified {
493			// Return any updated validators from the 304 response so the caller can persist a
494			// rotated ETag or Last-Modified without treating the bundle as changed.
495			return Result{
496				Unchanged:    true,
497				ETag:         respHeaders.Get(headerETag),
498				LastModified: respHeaders.Get(headerLastModified),
499			}, nil
500		}
501	
502		actualChecksum := ComputeChecksum(data)
503	
504		if req.VerifyChecksum {
505			checksumURL := req.URL + ".sha256"
506			checksumData, _, _, err := doGetWithHeaders(ctx, client, checksumURL, req.Auth, nil, http.StatusOK)
507			if err != nil {
508				return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to fetch bundle checksum from %s: %w", checksumURL, err)
509			}
510			fields := strings.Fields(string(checksumData))
511			if len(fields) == 0 {
512				return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("checksum file at %s is empty", checksumURL)
513			}
514			expectedChecksum := strings.ToLower(fields[0])
515			if _, err := hex.DecodeString(expectedChecksum); err != nil || len(expectedChecksum) != 64 {
516				return Result{}, fmt.Errorf(
517					"checksum file at %s contains invalid checksum %q: expected 64 hex characters",
518					checksumURL, fields[0],
519				)
520			}
521			if expectedChecksum != actualChecksum {
522				return Result{}, &nonTransientError{
523					err: fmt.Errorf("bundle checksum mismatch: expected %s, got %s", expectedChecksum, actualChecksum),
524				}
525			}
526		}
527	
528		return Result{
529			Data:         data,
530			Checksum:     actualChecksum,
531			ETag:         respHeaders.Get(headerETag),
532			LastModified: respHeaders.Get(headerLastModified),
533		}, nil
534	}

536	// nimBundleItem is a single entry in the NIM bundles API response.
537	type nimBundleItem struct {
538		Content  string `json:"content"`
539		Metadata struct {
540			Hash      string `json:"hash"`
541			Created   string `json:"created"`
542			PolicyUID string `json:"policyUID"`
543		} `json:"metadata"`
544	}
545	
546	// nimResponse is the JSON envelope returned by the NIM bundles API.
547	type nimResponse struct {
548		Items []nimBundleItem `json:"items"`
549	}
550	
551	// latestNIMItem returns the item with the most recent metadata.created timestamp.
552	// The NIM bundles API may return multiple compiled bundles for the same policy name
553	// (one per compilation) in an undefined order. This function selects the most recently
554	// compiled bundle by comparing RFC 3339 timestamps.
555	// Falls back to the last item if no timestamps are parseable.
556	func latestNIMItem(items []nimBundleItem) nimBundleItem {
557		best := len(items) - 1
558		var bestTime time.Time
559	
560		for i, item := range items {
561			t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, item.Metadata.Created)
562			if err != nil {
563				continue
564			}
565			if t.After(bestTime) {
566				bestTime = t
567				best = i
568			}
569		}
570	
571		return items[best]
572	}
573	
574	// fetchNIM calls the NIM security policies bundles API and decodes the bundle from the response.
575	//
576	// When a request targets a policy by name, and not by PolicyUID, NIM may return multiple compilations.
577	// This results in downloading the content multiple past compilations.
578	// To avoid this, a lightweight metadata-only request is issued first to identify the most recently
579	// compiled bundle via its created timestamp.
580	// This ensures a single bundle is fetched by its policyUID.
581	func fetchNIM(ctx context.Context, client *http.Client, req Request, logger logr.Logger) (Result, error) {
582		policyUID := req.NIM.PolicyUID
583	
584		// When fetching by policyName, resolve to the latest compilation's policyUID first.
585		if policyUID == "" {
586			var err error
587			policyUID, err = resolveLatestNIMPolicyUID(ctx, client, req, logger)
588			if err != nil {
589				return Result{}, err
590			}
591		}
592	
593		logger.V(1).Info("Fetching NIM bundle by policyUID", "policyUID", policyUID)
594	
595		return fetchNIMByUID(ctx, client, req, policyUID)
596	}
597	
598	// resolveLatestNIMPolicyUID performs a metadata-only NIM request to find all compilations for the
599	// given policy name, then returns the policyUID of the most recently compiled bundle.
600	func resolveLatestNIMPolicyUID(
601		ctx context.Context,
602		client *http.Client,
603		req Request,
604		logger logr.Logger,
605	) (string, error) {
606		metadataURL, err := buildNIMMetadataURL(req.URL, req.PolicyName, "")
607		if err != nil {
608			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to build NIM metadata URL: %w", err)
609		}
610	
611		body, err := doGet(ctx, client, metadataURL, req.Auth)
612		if err != nil {
613			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to fetch NIM bundle metadata: %w", err)
614		}
615	
616		var resp nimResponse
617		if err := json.Unmarshal(body, &resp); err != nil {
618			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to parse NIM metadata response: %w", err)
619		}
620	
621		if len(resp.Items) == 0 {
622			return "", fmt.Errorf("NIM metadata response contains no items for policy %q", req.PolicyName)
623		}
624	
625		logger.V(1).Info(
626			"Resolved NIM policy compilations",
627			"policyName", req.PolicyName,
628			"compilationCount", len(resp.Items),
629		)
630	
631		latest := latestNIMItem(resp.Items)
632		if latest.Metadata.PolicyUID == "" {
633			return "", fmt.Errorf("NIM metadata response contains no policyUID for policy %q", req.PolicyName)
634		}
635	
636		logger.V(1).Info(
637			"Selected latest NIM compilation",
638			"policyName", req.PolicyName,
639			"policyUID", latest.Metadata.PolicyUID,
640			"created", latest.Metadata.Created,
641		)
642	
643		return latest.Metadata.PolicyUID, nil
644	}
645	
646	// fetchNIMByUID fetches a single NIM bundle by its policyUID.
647	func fetchNIMByUID(ctx context.Context, client *http.Client, req Request, policyUID string) (Result, error) {
648		nimURL, err := buildNIMURL(req.URL, "", policyUID)
649		if err != nil {
650			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to build NIM URL: %w", err)
651		}
652	
653		body, err := doGet(ctx, client, nimURL, req.Auth)
654		if err != nil {
655			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to fetch bundle from NIM: %w", err)
656		}
657	
658		var resp nimResponse
659		if err := json.Unmarshal(body, &resp); err != nil {
660			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse NIM response: %w", err)
661		}
662	
663		if len(resp.Items) == 0 {
664			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("NIM response contains no items for policy UID %q", policyUID)
665		}
666	
667		// A policyUID-targeted request returns exactly one item.
668		item := resp.Items[0]
669	
670		data, err := base64.StdEncoding.DecodeString(item.Content)
671		if err != nil {
672			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to base64-decode NIM bundle content: %w", err)
673		}
674	
675		actualChecksum := ComputeChecksum(data)
676		bundleHash := item.Metadata.Hash
677	
678		// Verify the downloaded bundle against the hash reported by the NIM API,
679		// unless the caller supplied their own ExpectedChecksum via BundleValidation
680		// (the outer Fetch loop will check that instead).
681		if bundleHash != "" && req.ExpectedChecksum == "" {
682			if actualChecksum != strings.ToLower(bundleHash) {
683				return Result{}, &nonTransientError{
684					err: fmt.Errorf("NIM bundle integrity check failed: expected %s, got %s", bundleHash, actualChecksum),
685				}
686			}
687		}
688	
689		return Result{Data: data, Checksum: actualChecksum}, nil
690	}

692	func fetchNIMLogProfile(ctx context.Context, client *http.Client, req Request) (Result, error) {
693		compilerVersionURL := strings.TrimRight(req.URL, "/") + "/api/platform/v1/security/nap-compiler/versions/latest"
694		body, err := doGet(ctx, client, compilerVersionURL, req.Auth)
695		if err != nil {
696			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to fetch latest NIM NAP compiler version: %w", err)
697		}
698	
699		var versionResp NAPCompilerVersionResponse
700		if err = json.Unmarshal(body, &versionResp); err != nil {
701			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse NIM NAP compiler version response: %w", err)
702		}
703	
704		logProfileBundleURL := strings.TrimRight(req.URL, "/") +
705			fmt.Sprintf("/api/platform/v1/security/logprofiles/%s/%s/bundle", req.LogProfileName, versionResp.Version)
706		body, err = doGet(ctx, client, logProfileBundleURL, req.Auth)
707		if err != nil {
708			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to fetch NIM log profile bundle: %w", err)
709		}
710	
711		var resp LogProfileBundleResponse
712		if err = json.Unmarshal(body, &resp); err != nil {
713			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse NIM log profile bundle response: %w", err)
714		}
715	
716		data, err := base64.StdEncoding.DecodeString(resp.CompiledBundle)
717		if err != nil {
718			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to base64-decode NIM bundle content: %w", err)
719		}
720	
721		return Result{Data: data, Checksum: ComputeChecksum(data)}, nil
722	}

745	// n1cCompileStatusResponse is the JSON envelope returned by the N1C compile status endpoint
746	// (i.e. the compile URL without download=true).
747	type n1cCompileStatusResponse struct {

749		// Hash is the hex-encoded SHA-256 of the compiled bundle, present when status is "succeeded".

753	// fetchN1C resolves the N1C policy name (and optional version) to internal object IDs via the
754	// N1C policies API, then downloads the compiled bundle from the compile endpoint.
755	// Authentication uses the APIToken scheme rather than Bearer.
756	//
757	// Flow:
758	//  1. GET /api/nginx/one/namespaces/<ns>/app-protect/policies — find policy by name, get pol_obj_id.
759	//     The response also includes latest.object_id, so no second call is needed when policyVersionID is empty.
760	//  2. If req.N1CPolicyVersionID is set, it is used directly as pol_version_id (no versions list call needed).
761	//     Otherwise the latest version ID from step 1 (or findN1CLatestVersion) is used.
762	//  3. GET .../policies/<pol_obj_id>/versions/<pol_version_id>/compile?nap_release=<release>
763	//     Poll until status is "succeeded" (or fail on "failed"). The response includes the bundle hash.
764	//  4. GET .../compile?nap_release=<release>&download=true — download the binary bundle.
765	//     Verify it against the hash returned in step 3.
766	func fetchN1C(
767		ctx context.Context,
768		client *http.Client,
769		req Request,
770		pollDelay time.Duration,
771		logger logr.Logger,
772	) (Result, error) {
773		polObjID, polVersionID, err := resolveN1CIDs(ctx, client, req)
774		if err != nil {
775			return Result{}, err
776		}
777	
778		statusURL, err := buildN1CCompileStatusURL(req.URL, req.N1C.Namespace, polObjID, polVersionID)
779		if err != nil {
780			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to build N1C compile status URL: %w", err)
781		}
782	
783		compileTimeout := defaultTimeout
784		if req.Timeout != nil {
785			compileTimeout = req.Timeout.Duration
786		}
787		pollCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, compileTimeout)
788		defer cancel()
789	
790		bundleHash, err := pollN1CCompileStatus(pollCtx, client, statusURL, req.Auth, pollDelay, logger, "policy")
791		if err != nil {
792			return Result{}, err
793		}
794	
795		downloadURL, err := buildN1CCompileDownloadURL(req.URL, req.N1C.Namespace, polObjID, polVersionID)
796		if err != nil {
797			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to build N1C compile download URL: %w", err)
798		}
799	
800		body, err := doGet(ctx, client, downloadURL, req.Auth)
801		if err != nil {
802			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to fetch N1C compiled bundle: %w", err)
803		}
804	
805		actualChecksum := ComputeChecksum(body)
806	
807		// Verify the downloaded bundle against the hash reported by the N1C API,
808		// unless the caller supplied their own ExpectedChecksum via BundleValidation
809		// (the outer Fetch loop will check that instead).
810		if bundleHash != "" && req.ExpectedChecksum == "" {
811			if actualChecksum != strings.ToLower(bundleHash) {
812				return Result{}, &nonTransientError{
813					err: fmt.Errorf("N1C bundle integrity check failed: expected %s, got %s", bundleHash, actualChecksum),
814				}
815			}
816		}
817	
818		return Result{Data: body, Checksum: actualChecksum}, nil
819	}
820	
821	// pollN1CCompileStatus polls the N1C compile status endpoint until the compilation job succeeds or
822	// fails. It returns the bundle hash on success.
823	// Transient errors (5xx, network failures) are treated as "still pending" and retried after
824	// pollDelay. Non-transient errors (4xx) are returned immediately.
825	// kind is a short human-readable label used in log messages (e.g. "policy", "log profile").
826	func pollN1CCompileStatus(
827		ctx context.Context,
828		client *http.Client,
829		statusURL string,
830		auth *BundleAuth,
831		pollDelay time.Duration,
832		logger logr.Logger,
833		kind string,
834	) (string, error) {
835		for {
836			body, err := doGet(ctx, client, statusURL, auth, http.StatusOK, http.StatusAccepted)
837			if err != nil {
838				var nte *nonTransientError
839				if errors.As(err, &nte) {
840					return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to check N1C %s compile status: %w", kind, err)
841				}
842				// Transient error (5xx, network) — log and retry.
843				logger.V(1).Info("Transient error polling N1C compile status, retrying",
844					"kind", kind, "error", err)
845			} else {
846				var status n1cCompileStatusResponse
847				if parseErr := json.Unmarshal(body, &status); parseErr != nil {
848					return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to parse N1C %s compile status response: %w", kind, parseErr)
849				}
850	
851				switch status.Status {
852				case "succeeded":
853					logger.V(1).Info("N1C bundle compilation succeeded", "kind", kind, "hash", status.Hash)
854					return status.Hash, nil
855				case "failed":
856					return "", &nonTransientError{
857						err: fmt.Errorf("N1C %s bundle compilation failed", kind),
858					}
859				default:
860					logger.V(1).Info("N1C bundle compilation in progress", "kind", kind, "status", status.Status)
861				}
862				// Any other status (e.g. "pending") — fall through to wait and retry.
863			}
864	
865			select {
866			case <-ctx.Done():
867				return "", ctx.Err()
868			case <-time.After(pollDelay):
869			}
870		}
871	}
872	
873	// fetchN1CLogProfile resolves the log profile object ID via the N1C log profiles API (when needed),
874	// then downloads the compiled bundle from the compile endpoint.
875	// Authentication uses the APIToken scheme rather than Bearer.
876	//
877	// Flow:
878	//  1. If req.N1C.LogProfileObjectID is set, use it directly (skip the list call).
879	//     Otherwise, GET .../app-protect/log-profiles — find the profile by req.LogProfileName.
880	//  2. GET .../app-protect/log-profiles/<lp_obj_id>/compile?nap_release=<release>&download=true
881	func fetchN1CLogProfile(ctx context.Context, client *http.Client, req Request) (Result, error) {
882		lpObjID := req.N1C.LogProfileObjectID
883		if lpObjID == "" {
884			var err error
885			lpObjID, err = findN1CLogProfile(ctx, client, req.URL, req.N1C.Namespace, req.LogProfileName, req.Auth)
886			if err != nil {
887				return Result{}, err
888			}
889		}
890	
891		compileURL, err := buildN1CLogProfileCompileURL(req.URL, req.N1C.Namespace, lpObjID)
892		if err != nil {
893			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to build N1C log profile compile URL: %w", err)
894		}
895	
896		body, err := doGet(ctx, client, compileURL, req.Auth)
897		if err != nil {
898			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to fetch N1C compiled log profile bundle: %w", err)
899		}
900	
901		return Result{Data: body, Checksum: ComputeChecksum(body)}, nil
902	}

904	// n1cPagedResult is the common JSON envelope shape shared by the N1C list APIs.
905	// The generic parameter T is the item type in the page.
906	type n1cPagedResult[T any] struct {
907		Items []T `json:"items"`
908		Total int `json:"total"`
909	}
910	
911	// paginatedSearch iterates through pages of N1C list API results until pred returns true for an
912	// item or all pages are exhausted. buildURL receives the current (1-based) offset and page size
913	// and must return the URL to fetch. pred is called for each item; when it returns (result, true),
914	// iteration stops and result is returned. errMsg is used in error context strings.
915	func paginatedSearch[T any](
916		ctx context.Context,
917		client *http.Client,
918		auth *BundleAuth,
919		buildURL func(offset, limit int) (string, error),
920		pred func(item T) (result T, found bool),
921		errMsg string,
922	) (T, error) {
923		const pageSize = 100
924	
925		for offset := 1; ; offset += pageSize {
926			listURL, err := buildURL(offset, pageSize)
927			if err != nil {
928				var zero T
929				return zero, fmt.Errorf("failed to build %s URL: %w", errMsg, err)
930			}
931	
932			body, err := doGet(ctx, client, listURL, auth)
933			if err != nil {
934				var zero T
935				return zero, fmt.Errorf("failed to list %s: %w", errMsg, err)
936			}
937	
938			var resp n1cPagedResult[T]
939			if err := json.Unmarshal(body, &resp); err != nil {
940				var zero T
941				return zero, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse %s response: %w", errMsg, err)
942			}
943	
944			for _, item := range resp.Items {
945				if result, found := pred(item); found {
946					return result, nil
947				}
948			}
949	
950			if offset+pageSize-1 >= resp.Total {
951				break
952			}
953		}
954	
955		var zero T
956		return zero, fmt.Errorf("%s not found", errMsg)
957	}
958	
959	// findN1CLogProfile pages through the N1C log profiles list and returns the object_id
960	// for the log profile matching profileName.
961	func findN1CLogProfile(
962		ctx context.Context,
963		client *http.Client,
964		baseURL, namespace, profileName string,
965		auth *BundleAuth,
966	) (string, error) {
967		result, err := paginatedSearch(
968			ctx, client, auth,
969			func(offset, limit int) (string, error) {
970				return buildN1CLogProfilesURL(baseURL, namespace, offset, limit)
971			},
972			func(item n1cLogProfileItem) (n1cLogProfileItem, bool) {
973				return item, item.Name == profileName
974			},
975			fmt.Sprintf("N1C log profiles (namespace=%q, name=%q)", namespace, profileName),
976		)
977		if err != nil {
978			return "", err
979		}
980		return result.ObjectID, nil
981	}

983	// buildN1CLogProfilesURL constructs the N1C list-log-profiles API URL with pagination parameters.
984	func buildN1CLogProfilesURL(baseURL, namespace string, offset, limit int) (string, error) {
985		base, err := url.Parse(baseURL)
986		if err != nil {
987			return "", fmt.Errorf("invalid N1C base URL %q: %w", baseURL, err)
988		}
989		base.Path = strings.TrimRight(base.Path, "/") +
990			"/api/nginx/one/namespaces/" + url.PathEscape(namespace) + "/app-protect/log-profiles"
991		base.Fragment = ""
992		q := url.Values{}
993		q.Set("offset", fmt.Sprintf("%d", offset))
994		q.Set("limit", fmt.Sprintf("%d", limit))
995		base.RawQuery = q.Encode()
996		return base.String(), nil
997	}

1019		base, err := url.Parse(baseURL)
1020		if err != nil {

1198		nimURL, err := buildNIMMetadataURL(req.URL, req.PolicyName, req.NIM.PolicyUID)
1199		if err != nil {
1200			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to build NIM metadata URL: %w", err)

1206		}
1207	
1208		var resp nimResponse
1209		if err := json.Unmarshal(body, &resp); err != nil {

1211		}

1213		policyRef := req.PolicyName
1214		if policyRef == "" {

1225		}

1229			"policyRef", policyRef,
1230			"policyUID", latest.Metadata.PolicyUID,
1231			"created", latest.Metadata.Created,

1233		)

1266		}

1268		data, err := base64.StdEncoding.DecodeString(resp.CompiledBundle)

1351	// response includes the base64-encoded bundle; when false only metadata (including the hash) is returned.
1352	func buildNIMBundlesURL(baseURL, policyName, policyUID string, includeBundleContent bool) (string, error) {
1353		base, err := url.Parse(baseURL)
1354		if err != nil {

1356		}
1357		base.Path = strings.TrimRight(base.Path, "/") + "/api/platform/v1/security/policies/bundles"
1358		base.Fragment = ""

1360		if includeBundleContent {

1365		// NIM defaults startTime to now-24h when omitted, which silently excludes policies that
1366		// haven't been recompiled in the last 24 hours. Set it to the Unix epoch to return all
1367		// matching policies regardless of age.
1368		q.Set("startTime", unixEpochRFC3339)

1396	// doGet performs a GET request and returns the response body.
1397	// acceptedCodes lists the HTTP status codes treated as success; defaults to 200 OK when empty.
1398	func doGet(ctx context.Context, client *http.Client, rawURL string, auth *BundleAuth, acceptedCodes ...int,
1399	) ([]byte, error) {
1400		body, _, _, err := doGetWithHeaders(ctx, client, rawURL, auth, nil, acceptedCodes...)
1401		return body, err
1402	}
1403	
1404	// doGetWithHeaders performs a GET request with optional extra request headers and returns the
1405	// response body, response headers, status code, and any error.
1406	// acceptedCodes lists the HTTP status codes treated as success; defaults to 200 OK when empty.
1407	// For accepted non-200 codes (e.g. 304) the body will be empty and no error is returned.
1408	func doGetWithHeaders(
1409		ctx context.Context,
1410		client *http.Client,
1411		rawURL string,
1412		auth *BundleAuth,
1413		extraHeaders map[string]string,
1414		acceptedCodes ...int,
1415	) ([]byte, http.Header, int, error) {
1416		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, rawURL, nil)
1417		if err != nil {
1418			return nil, nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to create request for %s: %w", rawURL, err)
1419		}
1420	
1421		if auth != nil {
1422			switch {
1423			case auth.APIToken != "":
1424				req.Header.Set("Authorization", "APIToken "+auth.APIToken)
1425			case auth.BearerToken != "":
1426				req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+auth.BearerToken)
1427			case auth.Username != "":
1428				req.SetBasicAuth(auth.Username, auth.Password)
1429			}
1430		}
1431	
1432		for k, v := range extraHeaders {
1433			req.Header.Set(k, v)
1434		}
1435	
1436		resp, err := client.Do(req)
1437		if err != nil {
1438			return nil, nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("request to %s failed: %w", rawURL, err)
1439		}
1440		defer resp.Body.Close()
1441	
1442		accepted := acceptedCodes
1443		if len(accepted) == 0 {
1444			accepted = []int{http.StatusOK}
1445		}
1446		if !slices.Contains(accepted, resp.StatusCode) {
1447			err := fmt.Errorf("unexpected status %d from %s", resp.StatusCode, rawURL)
1448			if resp.StatusCode >= http.StatusBadRequest && resp.StatusCode < http.StatusInternalServerError {
1449				return nil, nil, resp.StatusCode, &nonTransientError{err: err}
1450			}
1451			return nil, nil, resp.StatusCode, err
1452		}
1453	
1454		// 304 has no body; return early with just the status.
1455		if resp.StatusCode == http.StatusNotModified {
1456			return nil, resp.Header, resp.StatusCode, nil
1457		}
1458	
1459		data, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
1460		if err != nil {
1461			return nil, nil, resp.StatusCode, fmt.Errorf("failed to read response from %s: %w", rawURL, err)
1462		}
1463		return data, resp.Header, resp.StatusCode, nil
1464	}
1465	
1466	// ComputeChecksum returns the lowercase hex-encoded SHA-256 of data.
1467	func ComputeChecksum(data []byte) string {
1468		sum := sha256.Sum256(data)
1469		return hex.EncodeToString(sum[:])
1470	}

1	package events
2	
3	import (
4		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types"
5		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client"
6	
7		ngftypes "github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/v2/internal/framework/types"
8	)
9	
10	// EventBatch is a batch of events to be handled at once.
11	type EventBatch []any
12	
13	// UpsertEvent represents upserting a resource.
14	type UpsertEvent struct {
15		// Resource is the resource that is being upserted.
16		Resource client.Object
17	}
18	
19	// DeleteEvent representing deleting a resource.
20	type DeleteEvent struct {
21		// Type is the resource type. For example, if the event is for *v1.HTTPRoute, pass &v1.HTTPRoute{} as Type.
22		Type ngftypes.ObjectType
23		// NamespacedName is the namespace & name of the deleted resource.
24		NamespacedName types.NamespacedName
25	}
26	
27	// WAFBundleReconcileEvent is injected by the WAF poller manager when a bundle that was previously
28	// unavailable has been successfully fetched for the first time.
29	// It signals the event handler to re-reconcile the affected policy so the Gateway config push proceeds.
30	type WAFBundleReconcileEvent struct {
31		// PolicyNsName is the namespace/name of the WAFPolicy whose bundle is now available.
32		PolicyNsName types.NamespacedName
33	}
34

820	func findWAFPolicyKey(gr *graph.Graph, nsName types.NamespacedName) *graph.PolicyKey {
821		for key := range gr.NGFPolicies {
822			if key.GVK.Kind == kinds.WAFPolicy && key.NsName == nsName {
823				k := key
824				return &k
825			}
826		}
827		return nil
828	}
829	
830	func (h *eventHandlerImpl) parseAndCaptureEvent(ctx context.Context, logger logr.Logger, event any) {
831		switch e := event.(type) {
832		case *events.UpsertEvent:
833			upFilterKey := objectFilterKey(e.Resource, client.ObjectKeyFromObject(e.Resource))
834	
835			if filter, ok := h.objectFilters[upFilterKey]; ok {
836				filter.upsert(ctx, logger, e.Resource)
837				if !filter.captureChangeInGraph {
838					return
839				}
840			}
841	
842			h.cfg.processor.CaptureUpsertChange(e.Resource)
843		case *events.DeleteEvent:
844			delFilterKey := objectFilterKey(e.Type, e.NamespacedName)
845	
846			if filter, ok := h.objectFilters[delFilterKey]; ok {
847				filter.delete(ctx, logger, e.NamespacedName)
848				if !filter.captureChangeInGraph {
849					return
850				}
851			}
852	
853			h.cfg.processor.CaptureDeleteChange(e.Type, e.NamespacedName)
854		case events.WAFBundleReconcileEvent:
855			// Guard against stale events: the poller may have been stopped (policy deleted) between
856			// when the event was queued and when it is processed here. Skip the rebuild if the poller
857			// is no longer registered — its bundle cache has already been cleared and a subsequent
858			// delete event will drive the correct config update.
859			if h.cfg.wafPollerManager != nil && !h.cfg.wafPollerManager.HasPoller(e.PolicyNsName) {
860				logger.V(1).Info(
861					"WAF bundle reconcile event for policy with no active poller, skipping rebuild",
862					"policy", e.PolicyNsName,
863				)
864				return
865			}
866			logger.V(1).Info("WAF bundle now available, triggering re-reconcile", "policy", e.PolicyNsName)
867			// Mark the processor dirty so Process() performs a graph rebuild even if this is the
868			// only event in the batch. Without this, clusterStateChanged=false causes Process() to
869			// return nil and the pending Gateway is never unblocked.
870			// We do not call CaptureUpsertChange here because that would overwrite the real policy
871			// object in cluster state with a metadata-only stub, corrupting the next graph build.
872			h.cfg.processor.ForceRebuild()
873		default:
874			panic(fmt.Errorf("unknown event type %T", e))
875		}
876	}
877	
878	// updateNginxConf updates nginx conf files and reloads nginx.
879	func (h *eventHandlerImpl) updateNginxConf(
880		deployment *agent.Deployment,
881		conf dataplane.Configuration,
882		volumeMounts []v1.VolumeMount,
883	) {
884		files := h.cfg.generator.Generate(conf)
885		h.cfg.nginxUpdater.UpdateConfig(deployment, files, volumeMounts)
886	
887		// If using NGINX Plus, update upstream servers using the API.
888		if h.cfg.plus {
889			h.cfg.nginxUpdater.UpdateUpstreamServers(deployment, conf)
890		}
891	}
892	
893	// updateControlPlaneAndSetStatus updates the control plane configuration and then sets the status
894	// based on the outcome.
895	func (h *eventHandlerImpl) updateControlPlaneAndSetStatus(
896		ctx context.Context,
897		logger logr.Logger,
898		cfg *ngfAPI.NginxGateway,
899	) {
900		var cpUpdateRes status.ControlPlaneUpdateResult
901	
902		if err := updateControlPlane(
903			cfg,
904			logger,
905			h.cfg.eventRecorder,
906			h.cfg.controlConfigNSName,
907			h.cfg.logLevelSetter,
908		); err != nil {
909			msg := "Failed to update control plane configuration"
910			logger.Error(err, msg)
911			h.cfg.eventRecorder.Eventf(
912				cfg,
913				nil,
914				v1.EventTypeWarning,
915				"UpdateFailed",
916				"None",
917				msg+": %s",
918				err.Error(),
919			)
920			cpUpdateRes.Error = err
921		}
922	
923		var reqs []status.UpdateRequest
924	
925		req := status.PrepareNginxGatewayStatus(cfg, metav1.Now(), cpUpdateRes)
926		if req != nil {
927			reqs = append(reqs, *req)
928		}
929	
930		h.cfg.statusUpdater.UpdateGroup(ctx, groupControlPlane, reqs...)
931	
932		logger.Info("Reconfigured control plane.")
933	}

938		k8sClient client.Client,
939		svc *v1.Service,
